selling-ai-websites.title: Selling AI Websites
selling-ai-websites.html:
Complete Guide to Selling Websites to Local Businesses

Important Mindset

This way of making money should not be your long-term business idea. This should be a way to:

  1. Develop your sales experience and build your MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) where you have very minimal work.
  2. Build connections with business owners so you can then sell them on other services like Google reviews management or AI receptionists as well.

Finding Leads

Method 1: Google Search (Most Common)

Go on Google and search in certain niches such as:

  • Roofing
  • Landscaping
  • Real estate agents
  • Plumbing
  • HVAC
  • Electricians
  • Auto detailing

Find the ones that don't have a website and cold call them.

Method 2: Facebook Groups (Higher Converting)

I would start with your local Facebook groups. This is a much higher converting method. You can obviously post on:

  • Facebook Marketplace
  • OfferUp
  • Local community groups

Method 3: Meta Ads

Run paid Meta ads targeting local business owners in these niches.

Method 4: Steal Leads from Others

Find ads or posts of people saying they will make websites, and look in the comments for people saying "very interested." You will see this a lot more than you would think, with the creator not even messaging or responding to them. Reach out to those interested commenters directly.

Pricing Strategy

Your Goal: Sell the website for $150-$1,000 upfront and charge a monthly maintenance fee of $10-$50.

How to Determine Pricing:

Just look at the business's traction:

  • If you think they're making a good amount monthly and it would not be a big financial burden, charge them more.
  • If they're a very small business, obviously charge way less.

Domain Purchase

This will be relatively cheap (usually $10-$20/year). You should just charge this in the initial price or add it as a one-time fee.

Domain Providers:

  • Namecheap
  • GoDaddy
  • Google Domains

Transitioning from an Old Website:

If they have an old, trashy website, you can either:

  1. Purchase a new domain and redirect the old one
  2. Use their existing domain and completely rebuild it

Payment Collection

In terms of collecting payment, you can do this on almost any site. I would suggest creating the invoice plus monthly fee on either:

  • Stripe (professional invoicing)
  • Whop.com (my preference)

I always suggest Whop when it comes to collecting money from a client, as I have never had a problem in terms of verifying identity or getting paid.

Building the Website (Less Than 5 Minutes!)

Selling websites is one of the easiest ways to make some quick cash with less than 5 minutes of work for building a website.

Recommended Website Builders:

  1. https://lovable.dev/ (HIGHLY RECOMMENDED - use this one)
  2. https://www.landingsite.ai/ (alternative option)

I would suggest Lovable by far as the AI takes care of almost all of what's needed.

The AI Prompt (Copy & Paste)

Use this prompt in Lovable or LandingSite.ai to generate professional websites instantly:

Create a professional, modern website for a [BUSINESS TYPE] business called [BUSINESS NAME] located in [CITY, STATE].

Business Details:

- Services offered: [LIST MAIN SERVICES]

- Phone number: [PHONE NUMBER]

- Email: [EMAIL ADDRESS]

- Business hours: [HOURS]

- Physical address: [ADDRESS if applicable]

Design Requirements:

- Clean, professional design with a modern aesthetic

- Mobile-responsive layout

- Hero section with compelling headline and call-to-action button

- Services section with icons and descriptions

- About Us section highlighting experience and expertise

- Contact form with name, email, phone, and message fields

- Google Maps integration for business location

- Testimonials/reviews section (create 3-5 sample testimonials)

- Gallery section for before/after photos or service examples

- Footer with business info, social media links, and copyright

Color Scheme:

- Primary color: [INDUSTRY APPROPRIATE - e.g., blue for plumbing, green for landscaping]

- Use professional, trust-building colors

Key Features:

- Click-to-call phone number button for mobile users

- "Get a Free Quote" or "Schedule Consultation" CTA buttons

- Fast loading speed

- SEO-friendly structure with proper headings

- Trust signals (years in business, licensed/insured badges, certifications)

Style:

- Professional and trustworthy

- Easy to navigate

- Clear hierarchy of information

- Conversion-focused design

Additional Elements:

- FAQ section with 5-7 common questions

- Service area map or list of cities served

- Emergency contact section (if applicable)

- Social proof elements (number of jobs completed, years in business, etc.)

How to Customize the Prompt:

  1. Replace [BUSINESS TYPE] with: roofing, landscaping, plumbing, etc.
  2. Replace [BUSINESS NAME] with their actual business name
  3. Replace [CITY, STATE] with their location
  4. Fill in their contact information
  5. List their main services
  6. Adjust color scheme based on industry standards

Step-by-Step Process

  1. Find a lead using one of the methods above
  2. Contact them and pitch your website service
  3. Collect their business information (name, services, contact info, photos if available)
  4. Purchase domain (if needed) - charge them for this
  5. Use Lovable.dev and paste the customized prompt
  6. Review and make minor tweaks (takes 2-3 minutes)
  7. Send them the preview link for approval
  8. Collect payment via Stripe or Whop
  9. Set up recurring monthly billing for maintenance
  10. Deliver the final website and connect their domain

Monthly Maintenance (What You Actually Do)

For the $10-$50/month fee, here's what you're "maintaining":

  • Hosting and domain renewal
  • Minor content updates (phone number changes, hours, etc.)
  • Security updates
  • Being available for questions
  • Updating photos or services as needed

Reality: Most clients won't request changes often, so this becomes passive income.

Scaling the Business

Once you have 10-20 website clients paying monthly:

  • You're making $100-$1,000/month in MRR with minimal work
  • You have relationships with local business owners
  • Now upsell them:
  • Google review management services
  • AI receptionist/chatbot
  • Social media management
  • Local SEO services
  • Google Ads management

This is where the real money is made—not just selling websites, but building long-term client relationships.

Pro Tips

  • Show them the website live first before asking for full payment
  • Use testimonials from previous clients to build credibility (This is why I say you may wanna use whop) You can make a bunch of fake reviews by making multiple whop accounts
  • Create a portfolio of 3-5 sample websites in different niches
  • Follow up with leads multiple times—most won't respond the first time

Common Objections & Responses

"I can build it myself"

  • "Absolutely! But most business owners I work with are too busy running their business. I can have yours done in 24 hours while you focus on making money."

"That's too expensive"

  • "I understand. What's your budget? Let's see what we can work with." (Then adjust pricing based on their business size)

"I need to think about it"

  • "No problem! I'm actually running a special this week—if you commit today, I'll waive the setup fee and you'll only pay the monthly maintenance. Can I send you some examples?"

"I already have a website"

  • "Great! Would you mind sharing it? Sometimes older sites aren't mobile-friendly or optimized for conversions. I can do a free audit and see if there's room for improvement."

Final Thoughts

This is a high-margin, low-effort side hustle that teaches you sales, client management, and digital marketing. Treat it as a stepping stone to bigger services, not your end goal. Build the relationships, deliver value, and scale into more profitable offerings.

start-here.title: Start here
start-here.html:
Before I start I 100% suggest you should not start an ecom/ branded dropshipping store without $3,000-$5000 usd that you are ok with potentially losing . This is because no matter if you read all of this information and even apply it, it will be very difficult to scale your first store. Obviously all the material and everything I state will drastically increase your chances of becoming successful in ecommerce but you most likely have no creative experience in terms of ads how to respond to emails and I'm going to do my best to give you a solution to every single problem that you will end up facing but that might not work for you. ## Welcome

Most people misunderstand Dropshipping and E-commerce, leading to the wrong mindset about them.

**## What is Dropshipping?**

**Dropshipping** is a fulfillment method where an E-commerce store doesn't keep the products it sells in stock. Instead, when a customer makes a purchase, the product is directly shipped from a third-party supplier to the customer, meaning you won’t ever have to see it or touch it. Dropshipping is a form of arbitrage. You sell products at a higher price than the supplier’s cost. The primary focus is on outsourcing inventory management and order fulfillment, allowing you to run a business without handling the physical products.

**## What is E-commerce?**

**E-commerce**, short for electronic commerce. So what does commerce mean? It is the exchange of goods and services between people or businesses. So by full definition the meaning of e-commerce is the trading of goods and services on the internet.

**## Table of Contents**

I just want to say that everything you need to learn to get to your first 5-6 figures is in **chronological order**. I made it that fucking simple for you guys.

Dropshipping is simple to understand but hard to make work, especially if you’re a beginner. There’s no get rich quick scheme, anyone telling you otherwise is just trying to make money off you. If you treat e-commerce as a side hustle instead of a real business, you’re gonna fail. Stay super hardworking and disciplined every day, and your life will change. Soak up all this knowledge. Your future self will thank you.

product-research.title: Product Research
product-research.html:
THE COMPLETE WINNING PRODUCT RESEARCH GUIDE

How to Find, Validate, and Launch Profitable E-Commerce Products

CORE PRODUCT CRITERIA

Every winning product must meet ALL of the following requirements:

1. Solves a Real Problem

Your product must address a genuine pain point or need in the market. The best products solve problems related to:

  • • Making money
  • • Relationships/attraction
  • • Weight loss/fitness
  • • Pain relief (especially back pain)
  • • Skin care and hair care
  • • Any product that alleviates discomfort or improves quality of life

2. High Profit Margins (Minimum 3-4x Markup)

Your product should cost you $10-25 to source and sell for $40-100+. Aim for a 4x-10x markup to account for:

  • • Advertising costs (typically 20-40% of revenue)
  • • Payment processing fees (2-3%)
  • • Returns and chargebacks (5-10%)
  • • Operating expenses

3. Small and Lightweight

Shipping costs can kill your margins. Choose products that are:

  • • Easy to package and ship
  • • Less likely to break during transit
  • • Affordable to ship internationally
  • • Not bulky or heavy (ideally under 2 lbs)

4. Evergreen (Year-Round Sales)

Avoid seasonal products unless you're running a short-term campaign. Your product should have an active market 365 days a year. This ensures consistent cash flow and allows you to scale without worrying about seasonal dips.

5. Validated by Competitors (Proof of Demand)

The product MUST already be sold by at least one competitor doing $300,000-$500,000+ in monthly revenue. Never try to create demand from scratch—leverage existing demand.

6. Strong Upsell Potential

Can you bundle or upsell complementary products? Examples:

  • • Selling glasses? Upsell a glasses case
  • • Selling skincare? Upsell a full routine
  • • Selling fitness equipment? Upsell a workout guide

THE 3 WINNING PRODUCT TRAITS

All winning products must meet these three core traits:

1. Wow Factor (3-Second Rule)

The product should immediately grab attention in the first 3 seconds of an ad. Most people's brains are fried from scrolling, so your product must visually stand out. If your product requires a long explanation, I wouldn't recommend it for beginners, since you don't yet know how to create high-converting ads.

2. Low Cost of Goods

The best products to sell are products that have high perceived value with healthy margins. Your product should have at least a 4x-10x markup.

3. Solves a Mass Appeal Problem

Here are criteria that will help you find a product that solves a problem:

  • • Does it solve an insecurity? (e.g., pain relief, weight loss, anti-aging)
  • • Does it trigger an emotional response? (e.g., excitement, humor, nostalgia, satisfaction)
  • • Does it improve quality of life? (e.g., automation, convenience, hands-free solutions)
  • • Does it appeal to impulse buyers? (low research required before purchase)
  • • Does it help people save money? (cost-effective alternatives to expensive solutions)
  • • Is it evergreen or a seasonal bestseller? (year-round vs. trending for Q4, summer, etc.)
  • • Does this product have a passionate audience? (e.g., hobby-based, niche-focused products)

NICHES TO AVOID

1. Overly Saturated "Viral" Products

Examples: Mini projectors, crystal hair erasers, shark slides. These products have been beaten to death and will require massive ad spend to compete.

2. Phone Accessories

Hard to justify pricing and weak perceived value. Customers can easily find cheaper alternatives on Amazon.

3. Adult Toys & NSFW Products

Facebook will flag your ads immediately. Not worth the hassle.

4. CBD, Alcohol, Tobacco, Vape Products

Strict advertising bans on all major platforms. You'll get your ad account shut down.

STEP-BY-STEP PRODUCT RESEARCH PROCESS

STEP 1: Pick Your Niche

Start with proven niches that have strong emotional hooks:

  • • Pain relief (especially back pain)
  • • Hair loss solutions
  • • Skin care
  • • Weight loss/fitness
  • • Making money/productivity
  • • Relationship/dating products

All of these niches are lightweight, have high upsell potential, and tap into deep emotional needs.

STEP 2: Create Burner Accounts & Train the Algorithm

Create separate burner accounts on Google, Facebook, and Instagram specifically for product research.

How to train the algorithm:

  • 1. Doom scroll on your burner accounts and find content in your niche
  • 2. Read comments on videos to understand customer emotions and concerns
  • 3. Search for hashtags associated with your niche
  • 4. Find top brands in your niche and interact with their content (like, comment, save)
  • 5. Do this for at least 5-10 pages/accounts per social media platform
  • 6. Repeat daily to keep the algorithm trained

The goal is to make the algorithm think you're obsessed with this niche so it feeds you relevant ads.

STEP 3: Find Viral Ads (The 2:1 Rule)

Look for ads that meet these criteria:

  • • At least 10,000 likes
  • • At least 5,000 shares
  • • 2:1 like-to-share ratio (shows viral potential)

When you find a winning ad:

  • 1. Like, save, and comment on the ad
  • 2. Click through to the website
  • 3. Add the product to cart
  • 4. Initiate checkout (but don't complete)

This signals to Facebook that you're an active shopper, which will trigger even more ads to be shown to you.

STEP 4: Validate the Product (MOST IMPORTANT STEP)

You want hard proof that this product is selling at scale ($300,000-$500,000+ in monthly revenue).

Method 1: Keywords Everywhere (Chrome Extension)

  • • Install the Keywords Everywhere extension
  • • Search for your product on Google
  • • Look for at least 30,000+ monthly searches

Method 2: Google Trends (trends.google.com)

  • • Enter your product keywords
  • • Look for steady, consistent growth over time
  • • Avoid products with declining interest

Method 3: SimilarWeb (similarweb.com)

  • • Find the #1 competitor in your niche
  • • Check their monthly website traffic
  • • Look for at least 300,000+ monthly visitors

CRITICAL RULE: The #1 mistake is trying to create demand in the market. The #1 way to make money is to use what's proven to work and leverage the demand that already exists.

PRODUCT RESEARCH METHODS

Method 1: Kalodata

Kalodata is a premium tool that shows you winning products, ad data, and sales estimates.

Link: https://www.kalodata.com/signup?tc=slastt

Method 2: Facebook Ad Library

Search for keywords that indicate high-converting ads:

  • • "50% OFF"
  • • "Shop Now"
  • • "Limited Time Offer"
  • • "Hurry Before It's Gone"
  • • "Buy 1 Get 1 Free"

Link: https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/

Method 3: TikTok/Instagram Scroll Method

Use your trained burner accounts to scroll through your feed and discover new products organically. This is the most time-consuming method but often reveals hidden gems.

UNDERSTANDING PRODUCT SATURATION

Determining product saturation is a skill that develops over time. It can be challenging to assess, especially when you're just starting out. However, as you gain experience and test more products, you'll become better at recognizing saturation levels.

Important Note: A product being "saturated" doesn't necessarily mean it no longer works—it just means it's validated. This means you'll need to compete on ad creatives and unique marketing angles to stand out.

How to compete in saturated markets:

  • • Create better ad creatives (unique hooks, angles, and editing)
  • • Target different audiences (age groups, interests, demographics)
  • • Offer better bundles or upsells
  • • Improve your landing page and checkout process
  • • Use influencer marketing or user-generated content

FINAL THOUGHTS

Product research is the foundation of a successful e-commerce business. Take your time with this process. Don't rush into launching a product just because it "looks cool." Follow the validation steps, ensure there's proven demand, and make sure the margins make sense.

Remember: It all comes down to how you push out your product after validation and figuring out what's going right and what's going wrong through testing and iteration.

Success Formula:

  • 1. Find a validated product with proven demand
  • 2. Source it at a price that allows for 4x-10x markup
  • 3. Create compelling ad creatives that stand out
  • 4. Test, analyze, and optimize relentlessly
  • 5. Scale what works and kill what doesn't

Good luck, and remember: the market rewards those who validate before they innovate.

finding-a-supplier.title: Finding a supplier
finding-a-supplier.html:
Testing Phase (Before Scaling)

When you're just starting out, you’ll need to use platforms like AliExpress or CJ Dropshipping for product fulfillment. Private agents typically don’t work with stores that aren’t scaling yet.

Another option I used early in my journey was Private-Sourcing. Their pricing is slightly cheaper than AliExpress and CJ, but the downside is that you must preload funds into your account, and they don’t offer advanced fulfillment features.

These methods are temporary — use them only until you’re consistently getting around 20–30+ orders per day.

Scaling Phase (Private Supplier)

Once your product proves to be a winner ( I would not reach out to this site until you have at minimum 20-30 orders a day till you apply on their site l: https://fulfillme.typeform.com/slastt 

✅ Lower your product costs (higher profit margins)
✅ Provide faster shipping (4–12 days)
✅ Automatically handle bulk order fulfillment

How to Check if Your Product Is Sourceable

You can use 1688 to verify whether your product can be sourced at scale and to compare supplier pricing.

I’ve partnered with Fulfill, an American-run Chinese fulfillment company that offers more personalized, one-on-one service. They only work with clients processing 20–30 orders per day or more.

Once you reach that level, you can apply through their form to get started.

pricing-offers.title: Pricing offers
pricing-offers.html:
• How I Would Price A Product On My Store
  • Let's say the cost of goods plus shipping for my product is $9.95. Personally, I make sure my breakeven ROAS is around 1.2-1.3. A 1.4-1.5 breakeven ROAS is also fine to work with, especially since you guys will be dealing with higher costs for goods and shipping.
  • So the selling price would be $39.99
  • The website I use to find my breakeven roas is Breakeven ROAS Calculator

Here Are A List of Offers You Can Use For Your Store

  • 50% Off + Free Shipping (Classic offer)
  • Buy 1, Get 1 Free (Great for perceived value)
  • Buy 2, Get 1 Free (Encourages larger orders)
  • Buy 1, Get 1 50% Off (Good for higher-margin products)
  • Free Gift with Purchase (Increases AOV & customer satisfaction)

ad-creatives.title: Ad creatives
ad-creatives.html:
## 🎯 What Makes a Winning Facebook AD Creative?

A winning AD creative is an ad that grabs attention, holds interest, and drives clicks and purchases. Here’s exactly what makes an AD perform well:

**Strong Hook (first 3 seconds matters)**

Why? People scroll fast. If your AD doesn’t grab attention in 3 seconds, they move on.

✅ Good Hook Examples:

- "I stopped using regular moisturizer and switched to body butter instead. Here's why."

- "This serum didn't just give me a glow, it erased years of sun damage"

- "Why I quit applying lotion a year ago when I found this"

🚫 Bad Hook Examples:

- "This will change your skincare game!" (Too generic)

- "This product changed my life!" (Retarded)

- "Hey guys, let me tell you something..." (Wastes time)

Tip: Use shocking stats, questions, or pain points to create curiosity.

**Clear Problem & Solution (Storytelling Matters)**

Why? Customers don’t buy products – they buy solutions to problems.

✅ Good Example (Problem → Solution):

"If you struggle with sleeping, that's because most pillows don’t support your neck properly. That’s why we made the CloudNest Pillow, designed by chiropractors for perfect comfort!"

🚫 Bad Example:

"Our pillow is soft and high-quality!" (Doesn’t explain why it’s useful)

Tip: Show before & after transformations or real-life scenarios to make the problem feel real.

**Engaging Visuals**

Why? A great script means nothing if the video isn’t visually appealing.

✅ Best Visual Strategies:

- UGC (User-Generated Content) – People trust real users more than brands.

- Fast-Paced Editing – Change scenes every 3-4 seconds to keep attention.

- Text Overlays & Subtitles – Most people watch without sound.

- B-Roll Footage – Show product in action from different angles.

- Emotional Reactions – Faces with real emotions = more engagement.

🚫 Worst Visual Mistakes:

- Pixelated or low-quality footage.

- Long, static shots with no movement.

- Overuse of generic stock videos.

Tip: If you don’t have UGC, use TikTok, YouTube Shorts, or stock footage ([Pexels](https://www.pexels.com/), [Storyblocks](https://www.storyblocks.com/).

## 📢 4. Strong CTA

Why? If you don’t tell people what to do, they won’t take action.

✅ Best CTAs:

- "Click below to get 50% OFF – Limited Stock!"

- "Buy 1, Get 1 Free – Order Now!"

- "Get yours today before it’s gone!"

🚫 Worst CTAs:

- "Our pillow is nice and comfortable. If you want, you can check it out whenever you have time." (Retarded)

- "Visit our website if you want..." (Weak & non-urgent)

- "Maybe check it out?" (Too soft)

Tip: You can stack multiple offers at the end to make your CTA stronger (BOGO, free shipping, money-back guarantee).

## The Importance of the 5 Stages of Awareness for AD Success

Understanding where your audience is in their journey is critical to creating AD creatives that resonate. When you tailor your message to match their awareness level, your ads become more relevant and effective. If someone is completely unaware of their problem, an AD that directly sells your product will likely be ignored. Instead, you need to educate, nurture, and guide them through these five stages:

**Completely Unaware** – They don’t know they have a problem. Your ad should spark curiosity and introduce the problem.

**Problem Aware** – They feel the pain but don’t know solutions exist. Focus on highlighting their struggle and why it matters.

**Solution Aware** – They know there are solutions but haven’t found the right one. Position your product as the best choice.

**Product Aware** – They know about your product but need reassurance. Provide social proof, comparisons, and key benefits.

**Most Aware** – They’re ready to buy. Use urgency, promotions, and clear CTAs to drive conversions.

**Most of you are just starting out a store, so you are going to want to focus on creating top of the funnel ADs, which are compltely unaware and problem aware.**

fb-media-buying-intro.title: fb-media buying intro
fb-media-buying-intro.html:
## Beginner’s Guide to Facebook Media Buying

Before running ADs, you need to set up:

✅ Facebook Business Manager – The control center for your ads.

✅ AD Accounts – Create 3-5 per new Business Manager.

✅ Facebook Pixel – A tracking tool that helps Facebook find the right customers.

✅ Facebook Page – The “face” of your business for running ads.

2. **Understanding the Facebook Pixel**

The Facebook Pixel is like a smart tracker for your ADs. It’s a small piece of code you install on your website that helps Facebook learn who is interested in your product and optimize your ads to show to the right people.

Think of it like a personal assistant for your ads:

✅ It tracks who visits your website

✅ It sees what actions people take (viewing, adding to cart, purchasing, etc.)

✅ It helps Facebook find more people like your buyers

✅ It optimizes your ADs over time to get better results

🚨 IMPORTANT: Once you have a winning store, you should share your pixel with another Business Manager that you aren't going to use for running ADs so you don’t lose everything if Facebook disables your account.

facebook-ads-testing.title: Facebook ads testing
facebook-ads-testing.html:
## My Testing Strategy

Set up your campaigns properly. You can refer to <#1337741164987678802> for my exact setup.

Anyways, start small and test before scaling your budgets. Here’s how:

1️⃣ Start with $100 or $150 a day per product. (always launch ADs at 12 AM PST so that the campaign can spend evenly)

2️⃣ Test for 2-3 days (Spend $200-300 total per product).

3️⃣ How to monitor the data. Here's a checklist.

**After Launch (First 12-24 Hours)**

✅ Check if your ads are approved and running (No errors or disapprovals)

## Analyzing AD Performance

**Day 1: Analyzing AD Performance (After $100-150 Total Spend)**

The key metrics to check start becoming meaningful after spending $100-$150.

**Key Metrics to Track**

When you start running ads, monitor these metrics:

- CPM (Cost Per 1,000 Impressions) – Measures how expensive it is to reach people.

- CPC (Cost Per Click) – Tells you how much you’re paying per click (aim for under $0.50-$1.50).

- CTR (Click-Through Rate) – Shows how many people click your ad (aim for above 2-3%).

- ATC (Add to Cart Rate) – Measures how many visitors add your product to their cart. (aim for above 5-10%)

- ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) – Tracks how much money you make for every $1 spent.

**Here are some examples on how to analyze these metrics**

Low CTR and High CPC = Weak ads or lack of product-market fit.

High CTR and Low CPC but no sales = Poor product page, offer, or pricing issue.

Even if you're not profitable at first, it's okay don’t panic. Facebook needs time to optimize your ads. When running a product test, the main goal at the start is to gather data. Profitability in the first few days isn’t guaranteed, but if you are profitable early on, that’s a strong indicator of a winning product. If you have something among those lines above, keep running the campaign.

**Day 2-3 Analysis ($200-$300 Total Spend)**

By the end of Day 2-3, review your performance and make data driven decisions:

When to Optimize, Continue, or Stop:

- Breaking Even: If you believe in the product, experiment with new ad angles, creatives, or offers.

- Profitable: Keep running the ads and start scaling! (Refer to <#1337742299081343088> for strategies).

- Underperforming: If metrics show no improvement and the product doesn’t resonate with any audiences, stop the test and move on.

## Final Notes

Testing is all about gathering insights. Even if the product doesn’t work, you’ve gained valuable data to apply to future campaigns.

Another thing I want to say is that at the end of the day, your ROAS matters the most.

You can still be profitable even with high CPCs and CPMs if your overall ROAS is solid.

Use common sense, if it’s making money, keep it running. If not, try something new or move on. Stay consistent, trust the process, and you’ll find a winning product soon.

facebook-ads-scaling.title: Facebook ads scaling
facebook-ads-scaling.html:
Vid: https://youtu.be/swVwJlpDs3U 

## Facebook Ads Scaling Module

By the end of this module, you will learn how to systematically scale Facebook AD campaigns while maintaining strong ROAS

**Understanding Scaling Fundamentals**

Objective: Understand when and why to scale your campaigns.

What is Scaling?

Scaling means increasing ad spend while maintaining or improving ROAS. There are two types of scaling:

1️⃣ Vertical Scaling: Increasing the budget on existing campaigns.

2️⃣ Horizontal Scaling: Expanding into new audiences, creatives, or geographies.

When Should You Scale?

✅ When your ROAS is consistently above your breakeven ROAS.

✅ When your campaigns show consistent performance over 3-7 days.

✅ When you have strong creatives and a proven offer.

Scaling is not about just increasing budgets randomly. It requires a structured approach to maintain profitability.

## The Vertical Scaling Strategy

Learn how to gradually scale Facebook ad budgets based on ROAS.

Example on gradually scaling:

 

Day 1: 3.12 ROAS → Double budget (e.g., $100 → $200)

Day 2: 2.23 ROAS → Increase by 20-50% (e.g., $200 → $250)

Day 3: 1.24 (Breakeven ROAS) → Keep same budget and just let it keep optimizing

Scale budgets only when ROAS is stable. Avoid increasing budgets too fast, or you might trigger the "learning phase reset." I personally like to keep doubling my budgets until i get to $800, and then I start only doing 20% bumps end of day if I am above 30% profit margins.

## Surf Scaling (Real-Time Budget Scaling)

Only use Surf Scaling if your margins are 40%+ and your ad performance is stable throughout the day.

**What is Surf Scaling?**

If your ROAS is above 3+ during the day, increase the budget by 10-20% every 2-3 hours.

Example of Surf Scaling:

1️⃣ Morning (10 AM): your budget is $1500 and ROAS is 3.52 → do 20% and now budget is $1800

2️⃣ Afternoon (1 PM): your budget is $1800 and ROAS is 3.31 → do 20% and now budget is $2160

3️⃣ Evening (4 PM): ROAS = 2.85 → Keep budget, do not reduce or bump up anymore

4️⃣ At the end of the day (11:30 PM): I personally do not lower the budget, I keep it the same for the next day and I just let it optimize, even if performance drops slightly.

## Expanding to International Markets

Learn how to scale beyond the USA. First check with your supplier to confirm shipping availability. Only expand if your USA campaign has a stable 3+ ROAS.

1. Duplicate into a "Worldwide" CBO Campaign

Exclude the USA (to prevent overlapping bids).

2. Adjust Budget & Scale Gradually

Start with a $100 - $200 budget.

Use the same daily budget scaling rules above this module.

problems-you-will-run-into.title: Problems you will run into
problems-you-will-run-into.html:
## 🔁 Refunds & Exchanges

Step 1: Has the Order Arrived?

YES: Validate the customer’s claim to decide whether a refund is appropriate.

NO: Politely ask the customer to remain patient while the package is in transit.

Special Case: Marked as Delivered, but Not Received

If the package is marked as "delivered" but the customer hasn’t received it:

Ask the customer to check with their neighbors or local post office to see if someone else has it.

If the package can’t be located, review your Refund Policy to determine whether a refund is justified.

Once you’re making a few thousand dollars in net profit, hire a VA or IRL friend that has nothing to do in their life and train them to handle replying to emails.

You can hire a VA for less than $350/month for 40 hours per week.

## 🥊 Chargebacks

**What Are Chargebacks?**

Chargebacks happen when a customer disputes a charge with their bank for reasons like:

- Claiming they never received the product.

- Saying the product was defective or not as advertised.

**Why Are Chargebacks a Problem?**

Too many chargebacks can lead to Shopify shutting down your store or your payment processor banning you. Losing a chargeback means paying order value + bank fees.

## 🛡️ How to Handle Chargebacks

**Easiest Method: Use Disputifier**

Install the Disputifier app on your Shopify store.

If a chargeback is filed, the app automatically refunds the order before the bank processes the chargeback, saving you from penalties. This app can save you hundreds or even thousands over time.

**Manual Method: Appeal Chargebacks**

If your store is new and you don’t want to use Disputifier yet, here’s how to manually appeal:

Submit a detailed appeal explaining why you’re in the right.

Provide as much proof as possible to support your case.

**What Proof to Submit for Chargebacks**

To strengthen your appeal, include the following:

- Product Page: Show what the customer purchased.

- Shipping Times & Guarantee: Screenshot from your product page.

- Refund Policy and Shipping Policy: Clearly outline your store’s policies.

- Proof of Shipment: Provide tracking information and proof of delivery.

- Terms of Service: Highlight relevant sections.

- Customer Communication: Show messages with the customer (or lack of communication).

- Order Confirmation: Verify the customer’s details and purchase.

Banks prefer visual proof, so include screenshots of all the above. The more evidence you provide, the better your chances of winning.

## 🚨 Shopify Holding a 20% Reserve

If you're using Shopify Payments, you might encounter a 10-20% reserve hold on your payouts. This means Shopify will hold 10-20% of your revenue for 120 days (about 4 months) before releasing it.

**Why Does This Happen?**

- Scaling too fast 🚀

- Too many chargebacks or refunds in a short period

- Shopify deems your store as "high risk"

**What to Do If Shopify Holds 20% of Your Revenue?**

✅ Stick with Shopify Payments

Even with the hold, it's still better to keep Shopify Payments active since you can still:

- Run ADs & scale

- Pay for COGs (Cost of Goods)

- Process transactions smoothly (Shopify is generally more lenient than PayPal when it comes to payment holds.)

**How to Potentially Reduce or Remove the Hold**

- Improve Store Health: Make sure your customer service, shipping, and fulfillment are on point. Reduce refunds & chargebacks by setting clear expectations with customers.

- Contact Shopify Support: Email Shopify and explain that...

✅ You are actively shipping all orders on time.

✅ You haven’t received recent chargebacks.

✅ Your business is legitimate and scaling naturally.

If your store is in good standing, Shopify may lower the reserve percentage or remove it entirely.

**Should You Switch to Another Payment Processor?**

Yes, you could look for alternatives, but Shopify Payments is still the best long-term option. Other processors may also flag your store for sudden high volume and hold your funds, creating a similar issue.

## 🚨 PayPal Holds: What You Need to Know

Using PayPal as a payment method has its pros and cons. While it can slightly improve your conversion rate, it also comes with high risks due to frequent holds and restrictions on your revenue.

**Why Does PayPal Hold Your Money?**

- Low Monthly Limits When You Start:

PayPal doesn’t trust new sellers and will limit your withdrawals for the first few months. Your limit may start as low as a few thousand dollars per month, and any amount beyond that will be held until the next month.

- PayPal Freezes Funds If They Detect Risk:

If you scale too fast without an account history, PayPal might hold funds for 30-90 days. If you start receiving disputes or chargebacks, PayPal may request business verification. If they don’t like your answers, your money could be locked indefinitely.

- Worst Case Scenario:

PayPal may permanently restrict your bank account from being linked to another PayPal account. If this happens, you’ll have zero access to your funds until PayPal decides otherwise.

## 🛑 DMCA Takedowns on Shopify

A DMCA (Digital Millennium Copyright Act) complaint happens when a competitor claims you’re using copyrighted content (images, videos, descriptions, etc.).

If Shopify receives a DMCA complaint, they will:

- Temporarily take down your product page.

- Notify you via email.

- Keep your page down until the issue is resolved.

**How to Handle a DMCA Takedown**

If the DMCA is Legitimate:

✅ Remove any copyrighted material from your store ASAP.

✅ Submit a counter-notice to Shopify at legal@shopify.com confirming the removal.

✅ Shopify will review it and give you a date when your product page can be reinstated.

If the DMCA Is Fake (Competitor Trying to Take You Down)

Some competitors file fake DMCA complaints using fake names and VPNs to get your product page removed.

**How to Fight Back?**

1. Submit a Counter Notice – Reply to Shopify's email and state that the claim is false.

2. Explain the situation – Keep it professional and provide proof that you own the content.

3. Wait for Shopify’s response – Typically takes 1-2 business days.

**How Long Will It Take to Resolve?**

On average, it takes 1-2 weeks to reinstate your product page.

**Should you wait it out or move on?**

If this is a big winner you’ve been scaling for months, it’s worth waiting the 1-2 weeks. Use this time to make new ad creatives or even test another store.

Make sure you figure out what number this is for you and bet according to this. This is great as it is now allowing me to get $100-$350 down on Draftkings and around 100 on MGM. These all add up.  This is a must have especially when you have hard limits like myself. If you are having any difficulties downloading the tool please just reach out to me.

One thing that I have been using daily to maximize return is something called edge boost that guide can be found here. (this is free money and not taxable as technically it’s cashback) fdic insured and everything have made multiple 6 figure transactions and work with them personally as well.

Limits

There is really no way around these besides betting certain markets and staying away but you will eventually get all of your accounts limited. A few quick tips I would suggest to keeping your accounts a little bit longer is stay away from props if you’re not limited unless you are adding a second leg that’s also plus ev.I would just look for something that may be -360 and have a fv of -400 so it’s plus ev with higher chance to hit. You will see a lot of these with tennis and alt lines on Fanduel. You can also prime your accounts which is making your account look more like a degen(average losing bettor). That can be found here. I think this day in age priming is only worth it if your unit is over 2,000 and you’re fine with losing a few grand to start to get these accounts to vip and get deposit bonuses etc.

P2 breakdown: A p2 is using someone else's accounts to bet. You  must do this very carefully. Here are a few things to go over before starting to do this. The funding source for these people it has to be in the account holders name example I’m betting into dratings and need to deposit so you must use one of their cards or paypals to deposit into draftkings. I would either set up a joint bank account or send them money or have them fund it. If they fund the account they should onbvisouly get a bigger cut that’s on you to figure out. Please be careful with who you do business with and make sure it’s a trusted individual or somebody that you can get under contract. When betting don’t use the same wifi or device as yours. I suggest either ordering a Beelink mini pc to their house and remote desktoping to make the bets or ordering a iphone 11 at walmart and getting the boost mobile plan it’s like 140 dollars for a month of coverage with the phone and you can cancel whenever. In my opinion this is the best option. Do not connect this to your wifi as you don’t want the ip of your limited accounts to show as well.

Promo Betting: This is the easiest way possible to grow your bankroll. I would suggest betting promos every single day. This all ads up I would just go to an odds screen find best odds on that book and bet the bet that is for the promo. Example a nrfi/yrfi boost on Fanduel I would just go and see if fanduel has -115 when every book has -120, with the boost this will become ev. I believe promos even on limited accounts and small boosts limits is a free $1,000 dollars a month. Im telling you guys this all ads up.

Futures: For futures I originate around 60 percent of my futures from either modeling or sent by other sharp people. For the other 40 percent I just do ev to market. For futures for the top american sports I use circa sports as the sharp. To calculator the devig I use the devig tool up above. Circa is normally the sharpest book for futures as they are the only book that offers no on futures. For all my futures they will be posted in the server and what to play down to. Early in college sports on openers you will find a lot of value. I’m basically just betting on ball knowledge and nothing else. A good example of a great future I got on opener was clemson to win the acc at +285 to +265 and right now it’s +110 and will probably close at -110. I do have a twitter burner where I follow a bunch of beat reports for ncaff, wnba,nba, and nfl as well. I try and and go through all these pages once a day and it helps me figure out what lines to attack especially in the begining of the year.

MMA/UFC Pre and live For MMA I originate about 70 percent of my plays. I personally believe MMA is one of the very rare things you can bet on ball knowledge and watching tape and beat consistently and even day of. I watch a lot of these amatuer guys and work with a few other mma originators that are in the know of certain camps such as American top team and more. The best leagues for these are lower end leagues such as cage warriors, pfl, dana white contender series and more. I watch almost all of these guys fights every monday for lower end which also helps me beat openers very consistently if these guys ever go to the ufc which will take to win 1,000 on openers for ufc compared to having to burn accounts on oepners for pfl and etc. I believe that you can also win just betting ev on mma I would only suggest taking Betonline as the sharp for UFC fight week especially after Monday as this is when they bump their limits to max win 30,000 a click. For most markets whichever books is taking the most is the sharpest. In this case bet online is very confident in their pricing. I would just devig a lot of over under rounds on betoline to recs a lot of these will be ev right before the fight as the lines move a lot especially on the early prelims. There is a secret sharp for live mma and boxing which is draftkings. I would use draftkings as the sharp on a live screen and bet on other books if it is good enough ev I would only suggest this if it is good enough. I would suggest doing this on Ceasars not Fanduel because if your account is limited the timer takes way too long and makes it almost impossible to bet into it. I also do believe that you can bet live into these markets by betting on ball knowledge and live things you see. You have to be insaley quick and make sure you’re watching the fastest feed as well. For pregame Draftkings drops takedown props either the day of weigh ins or the day of the press conference. The under takedowns is the market that is off this is really kinda just betting off ball knowledge here but you get great clv I parlay these for account health reasons. You get 100-250 cents of clv very easily. There is currently no bot that has these that is not private but I’m working on one at the moment with a few friends. If I do add it I will add the webhook in the server.

College Baseball: College baseball is one the best sports in my opinion to easily beat. DraftKings will move the line about 4 hours before the game starts and the whole market will move. Draftkings will be off to the whole market make sure that you guys hit the market side as Draftkings is sharp and the whole market will move towards them. I have DraftKings move a run line two whole runs and it would even move on betonline which was taking 1k a click at the time during the tournament. The only problem is books don’t take a high limit. Fanduel takes to win $80-$120 on 10 percent limits.

MLB Live: MLB is the one most underrated sports in my opinion to bet on, especially live.

The main market that I focus on is first 5 totals and spread on Draftkings and mgm. For the sharp on this I just look for low holds on pinnacle or to market. These are very good ev props and they are low limits but these add up especially on a huge mlb slate. You will be able to make a few 100 on these a day and then after the first 5 end you will see a lot of very good totals and spreads to pinnacle on mgm dk and caesars. Make sure you’re betting on these markets after the first 5 innings so you’re not betting on opposite sides of a bet. Other things to focus live is when Fanduel is off market on a pitching prop and it’s a arb to the market they’re sharp on that. This will always be an over on strikeouts,. Hit the under on drfatkings,mgm, hardrock and etc if it’s a low hold or arb to Fanduel. Other things I look to attack live are draftkings ault over unders and run lines that are arbs to pinny or low holds. You will see odds all across the place some even all the way to -500 this will help with edge boost as it will go towards the playthrough and get you more cashback. I also use fanduel as the sharp on live aults for team totals, obviously if it’s off to pinnacle then hit the fanduel side but a lot of times fanduel will be offering arbs to the market, they’re sharp on these and I would bet the opposite side on recs such as draftkings,mgm, hardrock and etc.

For pre game MLB

Baseball pre game: For pre game MLB I use pinnacle as the sharp and whale orders on exchanges and pinnacle and bookmaker on openers. Also whales example say that someone has a 30k order the night before at an arb to the market this is most likely a syndicate or a very sharp originator and I will gladly tail them on recs and the market relatively moves to them as well. This is the same thing for props anything over 5k liquidity then tail the sharp on rec books as long as it its arb to market.

Market’s that I’m testing Goal scorer on Fanduel( should have update in the next 2-3 weeks)

Marketings I’m hitting and book: Market that I’m hitting which is manual is circa period lines to other books. I’m using circa as the sharp. The only book that ever is actually good is normally draftkings. I just pulled up the best line screen on the oddsview pre match . Almost all of my NHL market is on live.

Tennis: Tennis is something that I have not bet until we got to the dead period during NBA season. I had a lot of great success when I first started betting Tennis using the oddsjam ev tool and using pinnacle as the sharp. If you have challenger tennis I would go and camp the ev screen or arb page on oddsjam and and focus on total games for challenger throughout the day and huge heavy favorites that are arbs or low holds to pinnacle and get the playthrough for pinnacle.  A lot of these lines are available on draftkings limits around 30-130 on total games and 170-600 on ml and set spreads. These all add up though and also get you more cashback through edge boost especially the heavy favorites you can get a few grand down at times.

 NHL For NHL live sharp I just use pinnacle

Market’s that I’m testing Goal scorer on Fanduel( should have update in the next 2-3 weeks)

Marketings I’m hitting and book: Market that I’m hitting which is manual is circa period lines to other books. I’m using circa as the sharp. The only book that ever is actually good is normally draftkings. I just pulled up the best line screen on the oddsview pre match . Almost all of my NHL market is on live.

College Basketball Complete breakdown

Sharp breakdown on markets/ what I’m looking for. (mind you this is all live)

First half total points: For this the sharp is by far fanduel. This is the most profitable thing in terms of roi that I had betting live college basketball last year. In terms of what I’m looking for I’m looking for arbs that are between the alt over under for halves between Fandeul another book. Here is an example. Fanduel has the over 64.5 in the Duke vs NC state game at +178 and Draftkings has the under 64.5 at -135. I will be smashing the under 64.5 in this situation. One more example would be Fanduel has the under 67.5 in the Ohio state vs Purdue game at +135 and Caesars has the over 67.5 at -125. I will be hitting the Caesars bet in this example. Because I’m using Fanduel as the sharp. You will be able to get a lot of these bets and even middle a lot of these. Books are horrible at pricing this market.

In terms of halves bet (moneyline/spread for the half). Now I wanna start off with one thing: I'm very picky when it comes to these bets when it comes to betting the Fanduel side. It has been greatly off to hit the Fanduel. Not saying they’re the sharp on this selection but they’re definitely sharper than most books. A lot of the bets I just hit off the arb page and see which one is off more to market and hit that side. No real explanation besides that. Now with oddsview I did hit a few off the ev page. But only a small sample size as I switched from oddsjam to oddsview in March. I would mainly focus draftkings in both these markets they’re so bad at pricing these markets.

Pregame NCAA: Last year surprisingly I did not hit any pregame NCAA bets. This is something that I will be doing more this year. All 72,000 dollars of my college basketball profit was from live betting. In my opinion this is the best sport to live bet with the amount of volume on a weekly and daily basis. There are so many college basketball games it’s ridiculous the amount of volume you get in on a week. Even with bad limits this is the time to be betting.

Live player props: From my experience last year mind you I arbed these only cause I had no idea who the sharp was I would just say that Fanduel is the sharp. I would just go off Fanduel in this case. I really can’t provide any more info as betting college props became illegal in Ohio in January.

Totals/ Mainlines: In terms of spread’s a lot of the volume is again on Draftkings. They’re horrible at pricing lines. Especially if a team is a heavy favorite and starts down a few points. In terms of college spread’s I would only hit if they’re off by 2 points late in the game and 3 in the beginning. I say the same exact thing about total. In terms of who the sharp is I mainly focus Fanduel as the sharp for totals but for mainlines market average. There are times when Fanduel is completely off it’s actually very common you will see this a lot you can hit those lines. Sometimes they’re off 7-12 points. You will be able to tell this manually if it’s a Fanduel error line.

Xbet edge: This is one of the best edges out there on an offshore book and I have had no problems ever getting paid out. What the edge is that you’re getting half a run extra with 1 inning left in the game. Example the rays game is 5-3 going into the 9th with one whole extra inning. Every book has the total at 8.5 but xbet has the line at 9. This is a huge edge as you’re getting half an extra run

player-2-guide.title: Player 2 guide
player-2-guide.html:
P2s: Getting Started with a New P2 Account

If you're setting up a new P2 account, here’s a breakdown of what I recommend:

  1. Devices: Start by picking up a burner phone or a budget-friendly laptop. Personally, I use Oddsview, which is challenging to navigate efficiently in live settings, especially without a desktop. That said, it's totally doable on a laptop—it just helps me hit more bets and read lines more clearly when I'm on a desktop.
  2. Finding Gear: Check Facebook Marketplace for a decent used laptop, or pick one up at Walmart. For phones, I buy Boost Mobile iPhones—either the iPhone 11 or iPhone SE. After buying the phone, get the $40/month Boost Mobile plan.
  3. Setup Tips:
  • Do NOT connect your new phone to the same Wi-Fi used for your P1 betting activity.
  • Set up the phone and install a profit tracker app like Pikkit. Link all your sportsbooks to the tracker.
  • When using your laptop, connect it through the phone’s hotspot to keep your network clean.
  1. Clean Devices Only: Make sure neither your new phone nor laptop has ever been used with any sportsbook accounts before. They should also not have connected to any Wi-Fi previously used for betting.
  2. Depositing Funds: If you're using a friend’s or family member’s identity for the P2, be sure to use their bank account or card for deposits. Using your own can get the account flagged or shut down.

unit-guide.title: Unit Guide
unit-guide.html:
Every betting pick posted in this Discord will be tracked using units. If you're new to betting, a unit is a way to standardize bet size regardless of your personal bankroll—it simply represents the amount of money you risk on a single bet.

How Do I Determine My Unit Size?

Great question! Let’s say you have a total bankroll of $10,000 that you want to use for betting. A common approach is to set your unit size at 1% of your bankroll. In this example, 1 unit would equal $100.

caesars-guide.title: Caesars Guide
caesars-guide.html:
🚫 Markets to Avoid:

  • Live Total Games in Tennis
     Caesars has shown consistent issues with this market during live betting. While it's been exploitable in the past, it's now a riskier choice and should be approached with caution.

✅ Markets to Consider:

  • Pre-Game Tennis Lines
     These lines tend to be more stable and are a solid B-tier option for maintaining account health. They offer a good risk/reward balance and are less likely to raise red flags.
  • MMA Betting
     Highly recommended and considered an A-tier market for both profitability and account longevity. This market is great for consistent value without drawing unwanted attention.

Additional Notes:

  • With Caesars’ current limitations approach, it’s best to reserve your action with them for larger bankrolls—ideally when you’re working with unit sizes around $500. They appear to be more tolerant of higher-stakes betting and are more focused on long-term profitability patterns than on short-term high-volume activity.
  • Consider incorporating live totals and moneyline plays from Bart. These have proven effective in preserving account health and can be a valuable addition to your overall strategy.

Let me know if you'd like this version tailored for a channel announcement or formatted for a PDF guide.

4o

priming-guide.title: Priming guide
priming-guide.html:
What is Priming?
Priming is the process of intentionally making your betting accounts appear unprofitable or “degen” (short for degenerate). The idea is to make it seem like you’re not a profitable bettor, which can help your accounts last longer without being restricted.

From my experience, this strategy works best with the following books: BetMGM, ESPN, DraftKings, Kambi books (like BetRivers), Hard Rock, and FanDuel. I did somehow manage to grab vip on  Betrivers parlaying huge favorites on MLB by going down 2.2k. The account then lasted about 3 days on 500 dollar units live for tennis. Which was well worth it as I went up 9.8k in those 3 days.

How to Prime Your Accounts:
The method I use to prime accounts involves betting parlays with heavy favorites. Here’s how:

  • Place bets on heavy favorites and combine them into parlays.
  • Maintain a low account balance, depositing only as needed, since you’ll likely lose during this phase.
  • For new accounts, aim to lose $1,000–$2,000 before using the account seriously.

This approach has worked particularly well for BetMGM and Hard Rock.

Priming FanDuel Accounts:
For FanDuel, I recommend betting on obscure or low-value markets, such as alternate NBA player props, and combining them into parlays. An example might be an 11-leg parlay with poorly priced alternate player props that result in a heavily juiced +400 payout. This strategy makes your account appear less threatening to traders.
